chunk_summary: This section discusses hemoglobin thresholds and severity classification in anemia management under India's Anemia Mukt Bharat program, highlighting WHO 2024 updates, clinical care implications, and national survey practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the updated WHO 2024 hemoglobin thresholds and their implications for anemia classification.
Learn how to apply severity classification for anemia in clinical and programmatic settings.
Recognize the importance of consistent frameworks in anemia prevalence reporting and quality improvement.
Identify clinical signs requiring urgent referral in severe anemia cases.
key_facts: WHO 2024 hemoglobin thresholds: <10.5 g/dL for 6–23 months, <11.0 g/dL for 24–59 months.
National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5) Phase 2 reports 67.1% anemia prevalence in children 6–59 months using older thresholds (<11 g/dL).
Hemoglobin thresholds influence school screening, referral urgency, and program dashboards in India.
Severe anemia with symptoms like tachycardia, respiratory distress, or shock requires urgent stabilization and referral.
Framework consistency is critical for comparing anemia prevalence across time periods.
reference_quote: WHO updated hemoglobin cutoffs in 2024, introducing separate thresholds for 6–23 months (<10.5 g/dL) versus 24–59 months (<11.0 g/dL) and updating severity bands.
document_key_topics: National anemia prevalence reporting in India
